message:
fix(cargo_metadata): concurrent pipe drain to unblock >64KB metadata output

`invoke_cargo_metadata` spawned `cargo metadata --format-version 1` and
polled `try_wait()` in a loop, then called `wait_with_output()` at the
end to collect stdout/stderr. For any project whose metadata JSON
exceeded the OS pipe buffer (~64KB on Linux) the child blocked on
`write()` to a full stdout pipe, never exited, and the timeout fired at
30s — even though the metadata itself was cheap to compute.

The RCH workspace's own metadata is roughly 2.5MB, so this function
timed out for *every* caller inside its own repo, leaking the child
process on the way out.

Fix: drain stdout and stderr concurrently in background threads via
`std::io::Read::read_to_end`, started immediately after spawn. The
main thread still polls `try_wait()` to enforce the 30s deadline.
Pipe capacity never fills because the drain threads keep reading.
On timeout we kill the child (which closes the pipes), reap the
child process, and then join the drain threads (which see EOF and
exit). On the happy path, `wait()` then `join()` hands us the
already-drained byte buffers.

Also:
  - stdout/stderr are taken from the child with `.take().expect(...)`
    since we spawned with `.stdout(Piped()).stderr(Piped())`; this is
    infallible.
  - Replaces the previous `wait_with_output()` (which re-consumed the
    pipes we just took) with an explicit `wait()` for the exit status
    and `thread.join()` for the drained bytes.
  - Join failures surface as `MetadataInvocationFailure` with
    `"stdout drain thread panicked"` / `"stderr drain thread panicked"`
    — belt-and-braces since the thread bodies only propagate
    `io::Error`.

Regression test: `test_invoke_cargo_metadata_handles_large_output`
fabricates a temp Cargo project with ~54 crate-deps ("*") — enough to
push `cargo metadata` stdout past 64KB — and asserts:

  - elapsed time < 30s timeout (the deadlock path always hit 30s)
  - resulting JSON is >64KB (proves we exercised the deadlock window)
  - JSON is well-formed (starts with '{')

Skips offline/network-failure errors since the test only targets the
deadlock regression, not resolver outcomes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
